question_text: A scientist has two identical containers. Container A holds hydrogen gas and Container B holds oxygen gas, both at the same temperature and pressure. Which statement is correct and why?

options.label: A
options.text: Container B has more gas molecules because oxygen is heavier

options.label: B
options.text: Container A has more gas molecules because hydrogen is lighter

options.label: C
options.text: Both containers have the same number of gas molecules because they are at the same temperature and pressure

options.label: D
options.text: Container A has fewer gas molecules because hydrogen molecules are smaller

explanation: Avogadro's Law states that equal volumes of gases at the same temperature and pressure contain equal numbers of molecules, regardless of the type of gas or its molecular mass. This is a fundamental principle of gas behaviour.
